title: Hosta 'June' with Japanese Painted Fern
description: Chartreuse and blue-green 'June' hosta paired with Athyrium niponicum. A classic shade border duo for zone 6 gardens. #hostas #shadegarden #hostagarden
alt_text: A clump of chartreuse Hosta June next to silvery Japanese painted fern in a shaded border

title: Blue Hosta 'Halcyon' with White Astilbe
description: Steel-blue 'Halcyon' hosta underplanted with white Astilbe chinensis in a woodland garden. Blooms in July. #halcyon #hostas #shadeborder
alt_text: Blue-green Halcyon hostas beside white astilbe plumes in dappled shade

title: Mixing Hosta Sizes in a Shade Border
description: Using giant Sum and Substance with medium blue hostas and small Gold Edger as edging. Layered texture for a north-facing border. #hostadesign #shadegarden
alt_text: Layered shade border showing giant yellow-green hostas behind medium blue and small gold-edged hostas

title: Ostrich Fern as Shade Border Backdrop
description: Matteuccia struthiopteris as a tall backdrop behind hostas and foam flower in Connecticut woodland garden. Spreads by runners. #ostrchfern #shadegarden #nativeplants
alt_text: Tall ostrich fern fronds arching behind a clump of blue-green hostas and white foam flower

title: Autumn Fern 'Brilliance' with Dark Hostas
description: Dryopteris erythrosora 'Brilliance' with emerging copper fronds paired with dark green hostas. Spectacular spring combination. #autumnfern #hostas #shadeborder
alt_text: Copper-orange emerging fern fronds contrasting with deep green hosta leaves in a shaded bed

title: North-Facing Border Layout Plan
description: Layout diagram for a 12 by 4 foot north-facing border. Tall ferns back row medium hostas middle edging plants front. Zone 6 plant list included. #shadegarden #gardenplan #borderdesign
alt_text: Garden layout diagram showing a north-facing shade border with labeled plant positions and spacing

title: Hosta Frances Williams Close-Up
description: Frances Williams hosta in mid-June with bold gold margin and blue-green center. Best massed under a canopy of mature oaks. #francewilliams #hostas #shadegarden
alt_text: Close-up of a Frances Williams hosta leaf showing gold margin and blue-green center with slug-resistant texture

title: Woodland Path Edged with Hostas
description: Informal woodland path lined with mixed hostas and native foam flower in a Connecticut backyard. Low maintenance once established. #woodlandpath #hostas #shadegarden
alt_text: A winding bark chip path flanked by mixed hostas and white foam flower in dappled shade

title: Felco No. 2 Classic Pruner Review
description: Long-term review of the Felco 2 bypass pruner. Replaceable blades and ergonomic grip. Worth the investment for ergonomic pruning. #felco #pruners #gardentools
alt_text: A pair of red and silver Felco 2 bypass pruners lying on a wooden potting bench

title: Felco No. 7 Rotating Handle Pruner
description: The Felco 7 with rotating lower handle reduces wrist strain during repetitive cutting. Good option for gardeners with limited hand mobility. #felco7 #gardentools #accessiblegardening
alt_text: Felco 7 rotating-handle pruner with red grip and silver blade on a green cloth background

title: English Wooden Trug in Willow and Chestnut
description: Traditional English trug made from willow slats and chestnut frame. Lightweight and good for carrying cut stems or harvested herbs. #trug #gardentools #woodentrug
alt_text: A pale willow and chestnut trug resting on a stone wall with garden scissors and twine inside

title: Burgon and Ball Kneeler with Side Handles
description: Foam kneeler with side handles helps when getting up and down in the garden. Important for knee and hip health during border weeding. #kneeler #gardentools #accessiblegardening
alt_text: A green foam garden kneeler with steel side handles sitting on a grass path beside a perennial border

title: Johnny's 4-Inch Soil Blocker
description: The Johnny's 2-by-2 soil block maker for starting perennial seeds. No plastic cells needed. Good for hostas and echinacea from seed. #soilblocker #seedstarting #gardentools
alt_text: A metal soil block maker pressing four square soil blocks on a greenhouse potting bench

title: Built-In Window Seat with Bookshelves
description: A built-in window seat with flanking bookshelves in a Cape Cod home. Good natural light and space for a reading lamp. #windowseat #readingnook #bookshelves
alt_text: A built-in window seat with cream cushion flanked by white painted bookshelves full of novels

title: Teak Garden Chair Under a Tree
description: A weathered teak garden chair positioned under a mature maple with a side table for a cup of tea and a book. Quiet outdoor reading spot. #gardenchair #outdoorreading #readingnook
alt_text: A teak garden chair with a small side table beneath a maple canopy with hostas at its base

title: Dormer Window Reading Nook Cushion Ideas
description: Upholstered cushion for a Cape Cod dormer window seat. Good firm support and washable cover for an everyday reading spot. #windowseat #readingnook #capecod
alt_text: A sage green cushion on a dormer window seat with a folded quilt and a library book on top

title: Pergola Reading Corner with Climbing Hydrangea
description: A small cedar pergola with a bench and climbing hydrangea for summer shade. Good for outdoor reading in the afternoon. #pergola #readingnook #climbinghydrangea
alt_text: A cedar pergola draped with white climbing hydrangea blooms with a bench and small table beneath

title: Hosta 'June' Chartreuse Cultivar in Morning Light
description: The chartreuse and blue margins of Hosta 'June' at its best in morning sun before the heat sets in. One of the most reliable hostas for zone 6. #hostajune #hostas #chartreuse
alt_text: A clump of Hosta June in morning light showing vivid chartreuse centers with blue-green margins

title: Hosta 'Sum and Substance' as Focal Point
description: Sum and Substance grows to 4 feet wide with golden-green leaves. Excellent as a specimen plant under a high canopy. Deer resistant. #sumandsubstance #hostas #largehosta
alt_text: A massive golden-green Sum and Substance hosta as a standalone specimen in a woodland clearing

title: Ostrich Fern Fiddleheads in Spring
description: Matteuccia struthiopteris fiddleheads unfurling in early May in a Connecticut woodland garden. Spreads to fill a shaded border in a few years. #ostruchfern #fiddleheads #nativeplants
alt_text: A cluster of bright green fiddleheads unfurling from the ground in a shaded garden bed in early spring

title: Echinacea purpurea 'Magnus' in Full Bloom
description: Magnus purple coneflower with flat ray petals that do not droop. Long bloomer from July to September. Excellent for pollinators and late summer color. #echinacea #coneflower #pollinators
alt_text: A stand of purple Magnus coneflowers with bright orange centers in a sunny perennial border

title: Coneflower and Black-Eyed Susan Border
description: Echinacea purpurea combined with Rudbeckia hirta in a sunny border. Self-seeds readily. Both attract goldfinches in late summer. #echinacea #blackeyedsusan #nativeborder
alt_text: A border mixing purple coneflowers and golden black-eyed Susans in a Connecticut perennial garden

title: Hosta Division for Plant Swap
description: How to divide hostas in early spring for a garden club plant swap. Best time is when eyes are just emerging. Pot up and label cultivar name. #hostadivision #plantswap #gardenclub
alt_text: Hands separating a hosta clump with a fork in early spring showing multiple crowns ready for potting

title: Teak Garden Bench Care and Oiling
description: How to clean and oil a teak garden bench to preserve the wood. Annual treatment with teak oil keeps it looking good for decades. #teakbench #gardenbenches #woodcare
alt_text: A teak garden bench being oiled with a cloth showing the grain of the wood in late afternoon light

title: Making Leaf Mold in a Wire Bin
description: Simple wire bin method for making leaf mold from fallen oak and maple leaves. Takes 1 to 2 years but produces excellent humus for shade garden mulching. #leafmold #composting #gardensoil
alt_text: A cylindrical wire bin full of autumn leaves in various stages of decomposition beside a shed

title: Christmas Fern in Winter Garden
description: Polystichum acrostichoides stays green through Connecticut winters. Reliable groundcover under deciduous shrubs where little else survives. #christmasfern #wintergarden #evergreenfern
alt_text: Dark green Christmas fern fronds remaining upright in a snowy Connecticut garden in January

title: Sussex Trug with Long Handle
description: The Sussex trug with a long handle for gathering cut flowers or harvested seed heads without bending. Light enough for prolonged use. #trug #gardentools #sussextrug
alt_text: A long-handled Sussex trug hanging on a garden shed wall with cut lavender stems inside

title: White Echinacea 'White Swan'
description: White Swan coneflower as a lightening accent in a mixed border. Pairs well with blue salvias and chartreuse foliage. Zone 4 to 9. #whiteechinacea #whiteswan #coneflower
alt_text: White Swan coneflowers with drooping white petals and green-bronze central cones in a sunny border

title: Adirondack Chair in Cedar for New England Gardens
description: Western red cedar Adirondack chair that weathers gracefully without painting. Low maintenance and comfortable for extended outdoor reading. #adirondackchair #gardenchair #cedar
alt_text: A silvered cedar Adirondack chair on a lawn with a garden border behind and a book left open on the armrest

title: Soil Blocking Mix Recipe for Perennials
description: The proper blocking mix ratio of peat compost perlite and garden soil for starting hosta and echinacea seeds in 2-inch blocks. #soilblocking #seedstarting #gardensoil
alt_text: Four two-inch soil blocks in a tray next to measuring cups of peat perlite and compost on a potting bench
